meta.title: ProxMenux Post-Install: Optional Settings
meta.description: Guide to Optional Settings in the ProxMenux post-install script for additional Proxmox VE features and optimizations.
meta.ogDescription: Guide to Optional Settings in the ProxMenux post-install script for additional Proxmox VE features and optimizations.

title: Optional Settings
intro: The Optional Settings category provides additional features and optimizations that you can choose to apply to your Proxmox VE installation. These settings are not essential but can enhance your system's capabilities in specific scenarios.

ceph.title: Add Latest Ceph Support
ceph.intro: This option installs the latest Ceph storage system support for Proxmox VE. Ceph is a distributed storage system that provides high performance, reliability, and scalability.
doesItems: Adds the Ceph repository to your system
Updates package lists
Installs Ceph packages using the 'pveceph install' command
Verifies the installation
ceph.howUse: How to use: After installation, you can configure and manage Ceph storage using the Proxmox VE web interface or command-line tools.
ceph.automates: This adjustment automates the following commands:

amd.title: Apply AMD CPU Fixes
amd.intro: This option applies specific fixes for AMD EPYC and Ryzen CPUs to improve stability and compatibility.
doesItems: Detects if an AMD EPYC or Ryzen CPU is present
Applies kernel parameter 'idle=nomwait' to prevent random crashes
Configures KVM to ignore certain MSRs (Model Specific Registers) for better Windows guest compatibility
Installs the latest Proxmox VE kernel
amd.howUse: How to use: These fixes are applied automatically and require a system reboot to take effect.
amd.automates: This adjustment automates the following commands:

ha.title: Enable High Availability Services
ha.intro: This option enables High Availability (HA) services in Proxmox VE, allowing for automatic failover of VMs and containers in case of node failure.
doesItems: Enables and starts the pve-ha-lrm (Local Resource Manager) service
Enables and starts the pve-ha-crm (Cluster Resource Manager) service
Enables and starts the corosync service for cluster communication
ha.howUse: How to use: After enabling these services, you can configure HA groups and resources in the Proxmox VE web interface.
ha.automates: This adjustment automates the following commands:

testing.title: Enable Proxmox Testing Repository
testing.intro: This option enables the Proxmox testing repository, allowing access to the latest, potentially unstable versions of Proxmox VE packages.
doesItems: Adds the Proxmox testing repository to the system's package sources
Creates a new file in /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ for the testing repository
Updates the package lists to include packages from the new repository
testing.howUse: How to use: After enabling this repository, you can update and upgrade your system to get the latest testing versions of Proxmox VE packages. Use with caution as these versions may be unstable.
testing.manualIntro: To manually add the Proxmox testing repository, you can use these commands:
testing.noteBody: $(lsb_release -cs) automatically detects your Proxmox VE version codename (e.g., bullseye).
testing.warnBody: Enabling the testing repository may lead to system instability. It's recommended for testing environments only.

fastfetch.title: Install and Configure Fastfetch
fastfetch.intro: This option silently installs and configures Fastfetch, a system information tool that displays system specs and a custom logo at login.
doesItems: Silently downloads and installs the latest version of Fastfetch
Allows you to choose a custom logo (ProxMenux, Proxmox, Helper-Scripts, Home-Labs-Club, Proxmology, or a custom one)
Configures Fastfetch to display "System optimised by ProxMenux"
Sets up Fastfetch to run automatically at console login
fastfetch.importantBody: If you connect to Proxmox via SSH, you should select the Proxmox logo or create a custom one using jp2a or img2txt. The other logos are generated using chafa and may not display correctly in a standard SSH session.
fastfetch.customBody1: To use a custom logo, place your ASCII art text file in: /usr/local/share/fastfetch/logos/
fastfetch.customBody2: You can create custom logos using tools like chafa, jp2a, or img2txt.
customItems: Keep the logo height to 35 lines or less to maintain proportions and fit in the terminal
Use chafa for color logos (may not display correctly in SSH sessions)
fastfetch.automates: This adjustment automates the following commands:

figurine.title: Install and Configure Figurine
figurine.intro: This option installs and configures Figurine, a tool that creates stylish ASCII text banners for your terminal, displaying your hostname in a visually appealing 3D format.
doesItems: Downloads and installs Figurine v2.0.0 from GitHub
Creates a welcome message that displays your hostname in 3D ASCII art when you log in
Automatically removes any previous Figurine installation if present
Sets up the welcome message to run automatically at login
figurine.practicalBody: When managing multiple Proxmox nodes in a cluster, Figurine provides an immediate visual indication of which node you're currently logged into. This helps prevent accidental commands on the wrong node and improves your workflow when managing multiple servers.
figurine.automates: This adjustment automates the following process:
figurine.outro: After installation, you'll see your hostname displayed in 3D ASCII art each time you log in, making it immediately clear which Proxmox node you're working on.

autoApplication.title: Automatic Application
autoApplication.body: These optional features are applied only when specifically selected during the post-install process. Each feature can be individually chosen based on your specific needs and preferences.
